nodes.label: AI as writing assistant for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: I go back and forth with the model

nodes.label: AI as tool for routine legal work
nodes.grounding_span: it is not 100% accurate and does miss key provisions

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: AI is not very good with legislation and tends to hallucinate sections

nodes.label: AI for administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI use for substantive legal reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: using it to make my day to day work faster, especially with administrative tasks

nodes.label: AI as enhancement tool not replacement
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human skills
nodes.grounding_span: I see AI as an enhancement not a replacement

nodes.label: Personal client communication as human domain
nodes.grounding_span: I like to speak in my own written voice and style

nodes.label: Professional competence
nodes.grounding_span: I will assess it's output critically like I would when reviewing work sent to me by another lawyer

nodes.label: Authentic personal expression
nodes.grounding_span: speak in my own written voice and style

nodes.label: Positive view of AI as thinking partner
nodes.grounding_span: the model explained it was exactly how I was intending the clause to work

nodes.label: Cautious trust in AI for legal research
nodes.grounding_span: I have felt frustrated with it when I have asked it points of law and it gets them wrong

nodes.label: Concern for junior lawyers relying on AI
nodes.grounding_span: This worries me as junior lawyers may use it for this and not know it is incorrect

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: it suggested a few things I hadn't considered but which will be worth mentioning

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and testing
nodes.grounding_span: I will get it to refine and edit the clause down for me until I'm happy with it

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting
nodes.grounding_span: I still do that work myself
